footer.ctaTitle: Ready to Start Your Next Chapter?
footer.ctaDesc: Sign up to experience full capabilities like orchestration, memory, and streaming generation, making AI your most reliable co-writer.
footer.ctaLogin: Already have an account? Log in
footer.tagline: An intelligent writing assistant built for novel creation, from world-building to chapter continuation, all in one place.

pricing.title: Clear & Transparent Pricing
pricing.subtitle: Choose the right plan for your writing volume. No hidden fees, upgrade anytime.
pricing.loadError: Failed to load plans, please try again later

1.a: The free plan is perfect for experiencing the complete workflow: orchestration, memory, and streaming generation. Billed by monthly AI tokens, you can upgrade when you run out.

2.q: How is it different from general conversational AI?
2.a: We bind to your novel's context, persisting chapters and settings. We support long-running tasks and transparent orchestration, designed for serialization rather than single-turn Q&A.

3.q: Can I upgrade or downgrade anytime?
3.a: Yes. Check your quota on the dashboard usage page; upgrades take effect immediately. The Enterprise plan supports custom quotas and private integration.

pricing.feasibilityNote: Not sure if it's right for you? Check out

guide.title: The Standard Workflow from Inspiration to Serialization
guide.subtitle: Four steps to build a reusable AI-assisted writing process, perfect for long-form and series works.

suitability.1: Daily/Serial authors needing stable continuation and memory alignment.
suitability.2: Long-form stories with complex world-building, fearing plot holes later on.
suitability.3: Writers who want to see the Agent's planning process, not a black-box output.

1.title: Establish Work & Settings
1.desc: Create a novel project, accumulating world-building, characters, and outlines. All subsequent operations by the writing assistant are based on this context.

2.title: Orchestrate Complex Tasks
2.desc: Continuation, revision, and setting retrieval can be broken down in parallel. The planning process is transparent, and long tasks can run in the background.

3.title: Streaming Generation & Polishing
3.desc: Text is generated in real-time and flows into the editor, supporting chapter-by-chapter rewriting, pacing, and word count constraints.

4.title: Continuous Iteration & Publishing
4.desc: Memory updates as you write. Export in multiple formats, with usage and quotas clearly visible on the dashboard.

about.title: A Writing Assistant Platform Built for Long-Form Creators
about.subtitle: We focus on novel scenarios—not general chat, but orchestratable, memorable, and hostable writing infrastructure.

1.title: Context First
1.desc: Hierarchical management of chapters, settings, and memory, avoiding 'forgetting the beginning when writing the end'.

2.title: Observable Process
2.desc: Planning, tool calls, and streaming output are visible to the user, keeping the creative process controllable.

3.title: Production-Grade Stability
3.desc: Long task hosting, resume on disconnect, and enterprise-grade secure transmission, designed for real serialization scenarios.

hero.title: Built specifically for long-form serialization,
hero.subtitle: World-building memory, transparent orchestration, streaming generation, and hosted continuation—from feasibility assessment to daily output, all in one place.
hero.scrollHint: Scroll down to see if it's right for you

demo.note: Interface preview — not your signed-in workspace
demo.caption: Orchestration and tool calls are animated previews. Register to use the real editor.

feasibility.title: Four Capabilities Supporting Serialization and Use Cases
feasibility.subtitle: Not a general chat box, but a productivity tool bound to your work's context, capable of long-term serialization.

serial.title: Serial / Daily Authors
serial.desc: Need stable continuation, controllable pacing, and low risk of writer's block. The assistant reads the previous chapter and outline, streaming text into the editor.

world.title: Lore Enthusiasts / Long-Form World-Builders
world.desc: Hierarchical memory of characters, factions, and foreshadowing. Retrieve settings before writing to avoid plot holes three hundred chapters later.

edit.title: Revision & Polishing
edit.desc: Rewrite, polish, and expand by chapter. The orchestration process is visible, allowing intervention anytime rather than a one-off black-box output.

context.us: Hierarchical persistence of characters, factions, and settings, staying aligned even after 300 chapters

orchestrate.us: Planning, breakdown, and execution are visible, making complex tasks controllable

stream.us: Text flows into the editor sentence by sentence, pause and rewrite anytime

resume.us: Long tasks hosted in the background, progress remains upon reconnection

story.introTitle: How Creation Happens in the Real Interface
story.introSubtitle: A three-act interactive demo: Context Alignment → Parallel Sub-agents → Streaming Generation

1.label: Orchestration & Planning
1.title: Before writing,
1.lead: Break down outlines, deduce pacing, and generate writing plans—let AI act on your intentions, no more blind boxes.

1.text: Deduce pacing and foreshadowing without taking up the main text area

2.text: Generate a writing plan first, then execute step by step

3.text: Clear status for each step, keeping long-term creation on track

2.label: Parallel Sub-agents
2.title: Complex tasks,
2.lead: Keep the main chat clean, put specialized work into independent panels, and merge back when done.

1.text: Proofreading and retrieval tasks run separately

2.text: Multi-thread processing without switching windows constantly

3.text: Summaries merge into memory for stabler continuation

3.label: Streaming Generation
3.title: Real-time text
3.lead: Words grow line by line, what you see is what you get; edit as you go, automatically saved upon completion.

1.text: Read as it generates, keeping up with the pace

2.text: Pause, follow up, and rewrite all on the same screen

labels.subagentThink: Check character cards against chapter tone
labels.subagentSummary: Character check complete — safe to continue Chapter 2.

think.title: Plan · Chapter 2 structure
think.prompt: Help me plan Chapter 2 — nail pacing and payoff beats before drafting.
think.think: Chapter 2: Silver Moon Forest opener validates loot drops, then trigger the server-unique enhancement stone; end on a cliffhanger.
think.output: Structure set: warm-up skirmish, then payoff spike — ready to draft.

orchestrate.title: Continue · Chapter 2
orchestrate.prompt: Continue Chapter 2 — align memory and the end of Chapter 1 first.
orchestrate.think: Before continuing, pull character memory and read the end of Chapter 1 for tone and foreshadowing.
orchestrate.output: Rain slid from his hair, each drop a bell on his heart. He breathed deep, fists tight — the first battle in Silver Moon Forest starts now.

subagent.title: Sub-agent · Character check
subagent.prompt: Run a sub-agent to verify character consistency, then continue the draft.
subagent.think: Keep the main session lean — delegate character proofreading to a sub-agent.
subagent.output: Character check done — memory synced. Ready to draft Chapter 2.

stream.title: Continue · Chapter 2
stream.prompt: Following the plan, draft the second half of Chapter 2 — focus on obtaining the enhancement stone.
stream.think: Pick up from the fight — describe the blue glow and system alert, highlight the server-unique payoff.
stream.output: As the stone burst into blinding blue light, runes crawled across his plain short sword. A system chime rang: [Server-unique mythic weapon acquired: Grip of Winter]

editorPrior: Tang Yun stood at the Silver Moon Forest gate with only a rusty iron sword in his pack.
"Continue Chapter 2" — he stared at the Agent panel, waiting for the next payoff beat.
editorStream: Rain slid from his hair; each drop rang like a bell against his heart.
He breathed deep and clenched his fists. Ten years — he'd finally waited for this day.
Shouts from the studio drifted in the distance while he looked down into his pack —
thinkLines: The user said "continue Chapter 2"; the prior text stopped at Awakening — bridge into Silver Moon Forest.
Suggested pacing: five mobs for guaranteed drops, then the server-unique enhancement stone payoff.
Risk: don't hand out too many mythic items at once — build contrast through volume.
editor.streamTail: The blade tip caught cold light, aimed at the sky.
editor.editorTail: Gear that had never appeared in any database glowed in the rain.
editor.userPrompt: Continue Chapter 2 into Silver Moon Forest — include a loot payoff beat.
editor.composerPlaceholder: Describe the scene or give an instruction…

capabilityMini.streamText: Rain slid from his hair; each drop rang like a bell against his heart. He breathed deep and clenched his fists.

prompts.hero: Continue Chapter 2 into Silver Moon Forest.
prompts.prd: From the outline, continue Chapter 2 — Silver Moon opener needs loot payoff; align Tang_Yun persona.
prompts.parallel: Proofread character cards and draft Chapter 2, then update memory.

cursor.narrative: Five mobs first to validate 100% drop pacing, then the server-unique stone; prose streams into the editor and memory writes back.

cursor.floatPrompt: Continue Chapter 2 with a loot payoff
cursor.prdPrompt: From the outline, continue Chapter 2 — Silver Moon opener needs loot payoff.

preview.title: Gods Have a Price · Chapter 2
preview.p1: Tang Yun entered Silver Moon Forest as rain slid from his hair.
preview.p2Before: Each drop rang like a bell against his heart. He breathed deep and clenched his fists —
preview.highlight: gear in his pack that had never appeared in any database glowed in the rain.
preview.streamLine: The blade tip caught cold light, aimed at the sky.

think.title: Transparent thinking, visible like chat
think.body: Before continuing, reason through plot: intent, context alignment, pacing and payoff — all streamed in the thinking panel.
bullets: think tool in its own panel, not crowding the manuscript
Structured Markdown output, easy to scan
Auto-collapses when done — focus on orchestration and drafting

orchestrate.title: Plan breaks down steps; tools line up
orchestrate.body: From ReadMemory through plan to WriteChapter — the whole chain unfolds in one orchestration layer: scroll as storyboard, creation as footage.
bullets: Live tool status: in progress / done
Args and summaries on one screen — traceable and reviewable
Long tasks can be hosted; resume syncs after disconnect

subagent.title: Split complex work; sub-agents run cleanly
subagent.body: Character proofreading and memory writeback run in nested panels while the main chat stays clear — no cross-spam.
bullets: Independent orchestration turns and tool trees

stream.title: Chapter prose flows smoothly — WYSIWYG
stream.body: WriteChapter streams longform into the editor line by line; pause, follow up, or rewrite anytime.
bullets: Auto write-back to chapter memory when done
